what_was_done:
- Built Torus-Pi Adapter Extension at ~/Desktop/torus-pi-adapter/pi-extension/ (8 files, 0 TS errors)
- Compared Torus memory vs Claude vanilla auto memory (semantic KB vs 200-line markdown notebook)
- Confirmed hookify is adopted marketplace plugin (Session 164), not custom-built
- Decided against replacing terminal history L2 with Claude JSONL transcript indexing (coupling risk outweighs simplification)
- Evaluated LanceDB as ChromaDB replacement: loses nothing, gains concurrent access + native TS bindings + built-in FTS. Decision: do it when triggered by real need
- Evaluated MySQL: rejected — no native vector search kills semantic mode
- Created full backup (1.4GB) and framework-only backup (99MB) on Desktop
- Catalogued all ~100+ Claude Code settings and env vars
- Enabled extra usage for 1M context window (opus[1m] / sonnet[1m])
next_steps: Migrate ChromaDB to LanceDB (when triggered — concurrent access or TS-native memory needed)
Build Claude API adapter for agent-bench (real API scoring)

ChromaDB concurrent access - tests skip when MCP running, correct behavior
